title: Thermodynamic Identification of the Internal Superconducting Phase Boundary in UTe$_2$ for $H \parallel b$
authors: Michal Vališka, Tetiana Haidamak, Andrej Cabala, Petr Proschek, Andreas Hausprug, Sergei Zherlitsyn, Vladimír Sechovský
abstract: The $H$--$T$ phase diagram of UTe$_2$ for magnetic field along the hard $b$ axis contains an unresolved internal boundary near $μ_0H \sim 14$--15~T, previously inferred from ac susceptibility and transport experiments but lacking thermodynamic evidence. We report ultrasound results for several elastic modes in an ultraclean UTe$_2$ single crystal with $T_c>2$~K for $H \parallel b$ down to 0.33~K and up to 18~T. A pronounced anomaly in the longitudinal $C_{33}$ mode, with a weaker response in $C_{44}$ and no resolvable anomaly in $C_{55}$, establishes this feature as a bulk thermodynamic phase boundary and reveals a symmetry-selective coupling to lattice strain. The phase line remains nearly constant in field near 14~T and terminates near 13.5~T and 1.25~K at a tetracritical point, providing the thermodynamic evidence for the fourth phase boundary in the $H$--$T$ phase diagram. The results constrain the order-parameter structure of the high-field phase and support field-induced multicomponent superconductivity in UTe$_2$.
summary: This paper provides the first thermodynamic confirmation of an internal superconducting phase boundary in UTe2 for magnetic fields applied along the b-axis. Using ultrasound spectroscopy, the authors observed a significant softening in the C33 elastic mode and a kink in magnetostriction at approximately 14 T. They identified a tetracritical point where the phase boundary terminates and demonstrated that the anomaly is symmetry-selective. This discovery confirms the boundary is a bulk thermodynamic feature and supports the existence of multicomponent superconductivity in this material.
main_problem: The existence of an unresolved internal superconducting phase boundary in UTe2 for magnetic fields along the b-axis lacked definitive bulk thermodynamic evidence.
main_result: Ultrasound measurements provide the first bulk thermodynamic evidence for this internal phase boundary, identifying a tetracritical point near 13.5 T and 1.25 K and revealing symmetry-selective coupling to lattice strain.
method: Pulse-echo ultrasound spectroscopy of various elastic modes (C33, C44, C55) and transverse magnetostriction measurements.
model_or_system: The physical system is UTe2, an orthorhombic heavy-fermion paramagnet and unconventional superconductor, studied in high magnetic fields (up to 18 T) and low temperatures (down to 0.33 K).
key_observables: Elastic constants (C33, C44, C55), relative frequency shift (delta f/f), and transverse magnetostriction (delta lc/lc).
important_parameters: Magnetic field H parallel to the b-axis, temperature down to 0.33 K, and the phase boundary near 14-15 T.
main_assumptions: The study assumes the use of ultraclean single crystals to ensure the observed features are intrinsic bulk properties.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the field dependence of elastic responses: (a) C33 softening and magnetostriction kink at 14 T, (b) C44 hardening anomaly, and (c) no resolvable anomaly in C55.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the unresolved phase boundary problem, describes the experimental setup using ultrasound and magnetostriction, presents the identification of the thermodynamic boundary via elastic anomalies, discusses the symmetry-selective coupling and the tetracritical point, and concludes with implications for the order-parameter structure.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Field dependence of the elastic response for H ∥b. (a) Longitudinal mode C33(H) measured with q ∥c and u ∥c, showing a pronounced softening anomaly near 14 T. The inset shows a selected ﬁeld window of the transverse magnetostriction ∆lc/lc for H ∥b. (b) Transverse mode C44(H) measured with q ∥c and u ∥b, where a weaker hardening anomaly is observed in the same ﬁeld range. (c) Transverse mode C55(H) measured with q ∥c and u ∥a, where no corresponding anomaly is resolved within the experimental resolution; the inset shows the measured sample and its dimensions in millimeters. Vertical scales are indicated by the scale bars in each panel.

Figure 2 summarizes temperature sweeps of C33, C44, and C55 at selected ﬁelds. The marked anomalies pro- vide complementary transition points for constructing the phase diagram in Fig. 3. The mode selectivity also has symmetry implications. In orthorhombic D2h symmetry, diﬀerent shear modes probe diﬀerent strain channels: C44 couples to εbc, C55 to εac, and C66 to εab [27, 28]. Recent ultrasound work argued that the low-ﬁeld superconducting state is single- component, with an odd-parity B2u state as the most compatible candidate, based on the absence of shear- modulus discontinuities at Tc [28]. FIG. 3. H–T phase diagram of UTe2 for H ∥b. Filled symbols denote phase boundaries determined in the present work, and open symbols indicate literature data from ac- susceptibility measurements by Sakai et al. [11]. The ultrasound-resolved internal boundary remains nearly con- stant in ﬁeld near 14 T and terminates near 13.5 T and 1.25 K at a tetracritical point (TetCP), completing the local phase- diagram topology by providing the fourth phase line required at the TetCP. The superconducting regions SC1, SC1+SC2, and SC2 are indicated.

FIG. 2. Temperature dependence of the elastic response for selected magnetic ﬁelds. (a) C33(T ) measured with q ∥c and u ∥c. (b) C44(T ) measured with q ∥c and u ∥b. (c) C55(T ) measured with q ∥c and u ∥a. Each panel includes the 0 and 18 T curves, and the marked anomalies denote transition points used to construct the phase diagram in Fig. 3. Vertical scales are indicated by the scale bars in each panel.
